channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Donald Trump is returning to the world stage. So is his trolling
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s recent dinner with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and his visit to Paris for the reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral were not just exercises in policy and diplomacy. They were also prime trolling opportunities for Trump.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell receives medical attention after falling
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) received medical attention after falling following a Republican lunch meeting Tuesday. A spokesman for McConnell said the GOP leader tripped after the lunch. "He sustained a minor cut to the face and sprained his wrist.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Biden stakes out economic legacy at Brookings as Trump readies takeover
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday made the case for the strength of his economic legacy as he prepared to leave office next month, arguing President-elect Trump would be inheriting a desirable situation and warning of the risks of the incoming administration’s proposals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Meet Natalie Harp, Trump's 'valuable resource' who lawmakers say is 'critical' to his operation
item.description: Lawmakers who communicate with President-elect Trump’s assistant Natalie Harp regularly told Fox News Digital that she is "critical" in ensuring Congress is a "real partner" for the incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden stakes out economic legacy at Brookings as Trump readies takeover
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday made the case for the strength of his economic legacy as he prepared to leave office next month, arguing President-elect Trump would be inheriting a desirable situation and warning of the risks of the incoming administration’s proposals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bragg lays out alternatives to dismissing Trump's hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg’s (D) office opposes dismissing President-elect Trump’s hush money charges but would accept halting the proceedings while he is in office, according to court documents made public Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mark Ruffalo presses Biden to act fast on 'forever chemicals' before Trump takes office
item.description: "The EPA has worked their butts off, against all odds, to get a drinking water standard on this particular chemical class," Ruffalo said.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump lawyers and aide hit with 10 additional felony charges in Wisconsin over 2020 fake electors
item.description: Wisconsin prosecutors have filed 10 additional felony charges against two attorneys and an aide to President-elect Donald Trump who advised the Republican in 2020 as part of a plan to submit paperwork falsely saying that Trump had won the battleground state that year.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Docs showing Biden admin called on banks to 'pause' all crypto activity confirms 'debanking' suspicions
item.description: Leaders in the cryptocurrency space are up in arms over documents that were recently uncovered showing the Biden administration instructed banks not to engage in cryptocurrency business.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan Democrats look toward a future that some hope includes Pete Buttigieg
item.description: MONROE, Mich. (AP) — In a packed union hall outside Detroit, a worker shifts the conversation from policy to the personal, quizzing Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg on his knowledge of Michigan, the state he only recently started calling home.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward Utah oil railway plan, but may not make broad environmental ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed to favor a Utah oil railroad expansion in arguments on Tuesday, but appeared more skeptical of putting strict new limits on a key national environmental law.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: The U.S. has had troops in Syria for the last decade to battle IS. The tumult following a rebel offensive that toppled Assad has raised fears of an Islamic State resurgence.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk warns Republicans against standing in Trump's way — or his
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — A week after President-elect Donald Trump’s victory, Elon Musk said his political action committee would “play a significant role in primaries.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, JULIET LINDERMAN and MARTHA MENDOZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats frustrated with Biden's 'lame duck' end to presidency
item.description: With 42 days until President Joe Biden cedes power, some Democrats are exasperated at how he is spending his final days in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Federal court blocks ACA coverage for Dreamers
item.description: A federal court in North Dakota has blocked Affordable Care Act (ACA) coverage for immigrants that came into the U.S. illegally as children, also known as “Dreamers.” U.S. District Judge Daniel M.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump voices support for RFK Jr's vaccine views, suggests Kennedy will research debunked autism link
item.description: “When you look at some of the problems, when you look at what’s going on with disease and sickness in our country, something’s wrong,” Trump said on "Meet The Press.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Manchin, Welch unveil bill placing term limits on Supreme Court
item.description: West Virginia Sen. Joe Manchin (I) and Vermont Sen. Peter Welch (D) have introduced a resolution to impose 18-year term limits on Supreme Court justices, which would require some turnover on the high court every two years.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Kinzinger has 'absolutely no worries' Trump will try to put him in jail
item.description: “First off, the executive branch can’t go after the legislative branch because we embarrassed him. That’s not a sin, that’s not against the law,” Kinzinger said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security beneficiaries at risk of delays amid GOP funding fight
item.description: Social Security beneficiaries could face longer wait times for service next year unless Congress agrees to the White House budget office’s request to increase funding for the Social Security Administration (SSA), the administration that runs the program is warning.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he can't guarantee tariffs won't raise US prices and won't rule out revenge prosecutions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump said he can't guarantee that his promised tariffs on key U.S. foreign trade partners won't raise prices for American consumers and he suggested once more that some political rivals and federal officials who pursued legal cases against him should be imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's 'Meet the Press' interview
item.description: Trump spoke with "Meet the Press" moderator Kristen Welker, and the two discussed Trump's plans for his first day in office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Talking Sense: Coming together to solve tough problems
item.description: Mariah Levison and Rob Fersh help people find consensus on how to solve divisive issues, ranging from prison reform to what art should hang in the Minnesota Capitol. Learn how they do it in this MPR News Talking Sense conversation hosted by MPR News correspondent Catharine Richert.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, Catharine Richert, Maja Beckstrom, and Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Trump criticizes Chronister after sheriff's withdrawal, days after praising him
item.description: President Donald Trump challenged reports of Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister's DEA withdrawal, saying he didn't want the sheriff days his cabinet days after nominating him.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s mother says The New York Times made ‘threats’ by asking her to comment on a story
item.description: A basic tenet of journalism — calling someone for comment on a story — was seen as a threat by defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth’s mother.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump reportedly considering DeSantis as replacement nominee for defense secretary
item.description: Several reports, including from the Wall Street Journal, have said Governor Ron DeSantis could become President-elect Trump's new defense secretary nominee. The news comes amid a growing number of allegations against Trump's original nominee Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Inside RFK Jr.'s nonprofit's legal battles over vaccines and public health
item.description: The nonprofit Children's Health Defense that Kennedy led has filed nearly 30 federal and state lawsuits since 2020, many challenging vaccines and public health mandates.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond, MPR News
